settings.closeToTray: Closing the window doesn't quit OpenBell - it keeps running in the system tray so scheduled bells keep ringing.
settings.outputDeviceDesc: Choose which speaker/output device bells play through. Leave on "System default" to let Windows decide automatically (recommended for most setups).
settings.lanSyncDesc: Share bell schedules between PCs on the same Wi-Fi/network - no internet needed. Pick "Host" on the main office PC that you edit schedules on, and "Client" on every other PC that should ring the same bells.
settings.lanHostHint: On each Client PC, go to Settings → Multi-PC Sync → Client, and enter this address:
settings.lanHostIpLabel: Host PC's address (shown on the Host's Settings page)
settings.lanConnectionOk: Connected! The Host PC is reachable.
settings.lanConnectionFailed: Could not reach that address. Make sure both PCs are on the same network and the Host PC's OpenBell is open.
settings.lanSyncSuccess: Synced {{schedules}} schedules and {{holidays}} holidays from the Host.
settings.lanSyncFailed: Sync failed. Check the Host address and that both PCs are on the same network.
settings.lanAutoSyncNote: This PC will also auto-sync from the Host every 5 minutes in the background. Note: sound files themselves aren't transferred - upload the same sound file names on each PC.

support.intro: Need help with OpenBell? Reach out to the developer directly using the contact details below.
support.helpBody: For bug reports, feature requests, licensing questions, or general support, email the developer or visit the website below. Please include your OpenBell version when reporting an issue.
support.footer: OpenBell is built and maintained by {{name}}.
